title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXXXIX. Lullabies.
content:
I won't be my father's Jack,
            I won't be my mother's Gill,
        I will be the fiddler's wife,
            And have music when I will.
                T'other little tune,
                T'other little tune,
                Pr'ythee, love, play me
                T'other little tune.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXXXIX. Lullabies."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
